headline: Penang Undersea Tunnel Project Stalled After RM305M Paid
context: The RM6.3B Penang undersea tunnel project remains at feasibility stage despite RM305M in advance payments to the contractor, with no construction start date confirmed.

cards.big: Penang paid RM305M for an undersea tunnel that does not exist.
cards.sub: After 10 years, the project remains at feasibility stage. The contractor has delivered studies, not infrastructure.

cards.big: The original EIA was approved in 2017 for a different alignment.
cards.sub: The current proposal differs substantially. DOE has not confirmed whether a new EIA is required.

cards.big: Reclamation linked to the tunnel project will destroy 800 hectares of seabed.
cards.sub: Marine biodiversity assessment for the revised alignment has not been conducted.

cards.big: The project cost estimate rose from RM6.3B to RM9.6B in 2024.
cards.sub: No revised financial viability study has been published.

cards.big: RM305M spent. Zero metres built. No EIA for the current design. This is not a project. It is a payment schedule.
Mega-projects need stage-gate reviews that tie payments to deliverables. Penang's tunnel shows what happens when money flows before milestones are met.
